ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002400 covers cheesecake as a general category. Silk Whisk Cheesecake is a specific café-style variant; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers cheesecake as a category. Specific silk/whipped variant not individually coded; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard café slice of Silk Whisk Cheesecake weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at EC Coffee Bar in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18147)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared cheesecake (NDB 18147 / FDC ID 172033). Silk Whisk Cheesecake is a café-style baked cheesecake with cream cheese, eggs, sugar, butter, and biscuit crust. Nutrient profile is consistent with this reference. Minor adjustments not applied; USDA values used as-is for per-100g basis.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 derived from published GI tables for cheesecake (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). Cheesecake GI is reported as approximately 46 (low) due to high fat and protein content slowing glucose absorption. Silk Whisk Cheesecake is a standard baked cream-cheese cheesecake; this analogous estimate is defensible. GL per serving = (46 × 26.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 14.4.

hypertension.guidance: A 120 g serving provides approximately 264 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and frequency, as regular consumption of high-fat, moderate-sodium desserts may contribute to elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a relatively low GI of 46, a 120 g serving delivers approximately 31 g carbohydrate and 22 g sugar, producing a glycemic load of ~14; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional dessert and account for it within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 120 g serving provides approximately 27 g total fat, 14.4 g saturated fat, and 126 mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dessert due to its high saturated fat content, which is associated with elevated LDL cholesterol.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Silk Whisk Cheesecake is a café-style baked cheesecake served at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street in Singapore, positioned within the Malaysian café dessert tradition. The cake is made from a cream cheese base blended with eggs, sugar, heavy cream, and vanilla, set on a buttery digestive biscuit crust and baked until silky smooth. The texture is characteristically dense yet airy, with a melt-in-the-mouth creaminess achieved through careful whipping of the cream cheese mixture before baking. The flavour profile is rich, mildly tangy from the cream cheese, and sweet without being cloying. Nutritionally, a 120 g slice delivers approximately 385 kcal, 27 g fat (of which 14 g is saturated), 31 g carbohydrate, and 22 g sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 46 (low), making the glycemic load moderate at approximately 14 per serving. Despite the low GI, the high saturated fat and sugar content make this a dessert best consumed occasionally. It is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Malaysian café contexts. Clinically, it is of moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to sugar load, and of high concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and cholesterol content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of cheesecake?
Is cheesecake high in saturated fat?
What is the GI of cheesecake?
Where can I get Silk Whisk Cheesecake in Singapore?
What are the ingredients in cheesecake?

confidence_reason: Silk Whisk Cheesecake is a proprietary café item from EC Coffee Bar, Singapore. No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD for this specific product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for commercially prepared cheesecake (FDC 172033), which is a defensible closest match. GI is estimated from published international GI tables for cheesecake (Atkinson et al., 2008). Confidence is medium due to the use of adapted rather than exact source data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172033, commercially prepared cheesecake) as no exact HPB or MyFCD entry exists for this proprietary café item. 2. GI of 46 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables for cheesecake; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 3. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (46 × 26.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 14.4 g. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, and all micronutrients are reported from the USDA adapted source; values are plausible for a cream-cheese-based baked dessert. 5. Halal status assumed based on typical Malaysian café context; not independently verified for this specific outlet. 6. Serving size of 120 g is an estimate for a standard café slice; actual portion may vary. 7. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002400 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are used as closest_parent matches for cheesecake category; no exact ontology code exists for this specific product variant.
